observations.recommendation: 2. The Inventory Committee plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) duly approved by the Mayor, containing the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of physical inventory;
3. The Inventory Committee submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory; and

4. Property records will be updated by the General Services Officer based on the result of the physical count and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the correct balances of PPE accounts.
11. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain necessary ledger cards according to MNGAS for LGUs, including the Construction in Progress Ledger Cards;

13. Instruct the aforementioned Offices to make a periodic reconciliation between the ledger cards and property cards to support the accuracy of accounts presented in the financial statements and make the data available at any given time.
14. The Municipal Engineer must provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

15. The Municipal Accountant must update and maintain the general and subsidiary ledger of LRN and diligently compute lapsing schedule and computation of the depreciation and impairment loss of each component at the end of the year and include it in the financial statement proper disclosure thereof.
16. The GSO must maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and prepare and submit the RLRN every end of the year; and
23. Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency must institutionalize the ICS;
24. Management obliged the Municipal Officers to act and do their respective duties and functions strictly in accordance with laws, rules, and regulations;
25. Direct the strict adherence in the application of internal control measures for the efficient and effective conduct of the operation of the Municipality towards development.
27. Submit at the start of the year, the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with complete details;

35. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer must effect a deduction of accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and withheld taxes and further remit them within the prescribed period of remittances.

38. We recommended that Management furnish a COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

40. Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.
41. We recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance with the provisions in IRR of RA 9184.

42. We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time under COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

43. We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand, and a representative of an NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; the opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.
44. Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.

45. Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring fewer costs, time, and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through a phone call, text message, or e-mail.

46. Must be made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with before the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by the administration.

47. The Chief Executive shall properly manage the proper utilization of public funds toned at the top to avoid the risk of misapplication of funds due to the withdrawal of funds from the bank upon credited by the bank and remiss in complying with the pertinent laws, rules, and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.
48. The Accountable Officer is directed to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents attached thereto after payments are made on such vouchers to preclude their possible re-use.

1.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Permanently appoint a competent General Services Officer/Property Officer
who shall be responsible for the PPE-related functions and records;
b) Direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all
properties of the Municipality; and
c) Require the submission of a complete report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

2.6 Thus, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate:
a) The Accounting Office to record and account for the purchase of
welfare goods, medicines, and other supplies and materials in a
perpetual inventory method;
b) The GSO and Accounting Office to implement internal control
measures to ensure that inventories are safeguarded from possible
wastage and misappropriation; and
c) The GSO to diligently prepare, maintain and update inventory records,
forms, and reports, and consequently reconcile such with the ledgers of
the Accounting Office.

3.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor strictly mandate:
a) That cash advances be strictly granted only to permanent, properly
bonded, and officially designated Accountable Officer and/or Special
Disbursing Officer;
b) The designation of a duly bonded Special Disbursing Officer that could
perform the necessary functions during exigent circumstances in which
the Accountable Officer may not be available;
c) That before the approval of payment of cash advances, the Accountant
ensures that applicable supporting documents such as approved
payroll for payment of salaries/wages, approved budget estimate on
current operating expenses, etc. are complete;
d) That cash advances are strictly monitored, liquidated, and reported as
soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served.

4.7 Thus, we recommended that the LCE mandate the concerned officials to
faithfully observe the following courses of action in the preparation and
submission of the financial reports and statements, viz:
a. Strictly adopt the prescribed templates provided in the NGAS Manual for
LGU, Volumes I and II;
b. Strictly indicate the necessary information and data on the financial
reports/statement such as detailed description, unit measure, report
number, and/or remarks; and
c. Strictly reproduce the required number of copies, for each type of report,
and furnish the same to the concerned offices.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
must affect a deduction of accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth,
Pag-ibig, and withheld taxes and further remit them within the prescribed period
of remittances. The prior year’s accounts must be reconciled, confirmed, and
remitted, if not, then initiate the applicable alternative action on that matter.

6.3 We recommended that:
a) Before payment of claims, Management must continue the strict
adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for the
documentary requirements needed on common government transactions
to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit; and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules, and
regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of suspensions and
disallowances (if any) under Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

1. The LCE immediately creates the Inventory Committee, composed of an adequate number of members, and complete the physical count in three (3) months or less and submit a report thereon.
The Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency;

5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountable officers to immediately submit the reports with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to substantiate the reliability and accuracy of accounts as presented in financial statements as of year-end.

6. We further recommended that concerned officials and employees involved in the financial operations of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions under existing laws, rules, and regulations.

7. We recommended that Management create a special account in the General Fund (SAGF) BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local sanggunian under Section 313 of RA No. 7160 to easily distinguish and track the utilization of funds related to COVID-19.
8. We recommended that Management prepare a monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and the status of implementation of PPAs.

9. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers related to the BGMC fund to avoid the receipt of notices of suspension and possible disallowances of the transactions.

10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning Development Officer to prepare the supplemental AIP for the COVID-19-related PPAs and the corresponding supplemental Appropriation Ordinance, duly approved by the local Sanggunian in support of the utilization of BGCM fund.
12. Immediately appoint or designate a capable and permanent General Service Officer who shall take charge of the duties and responsibilities inherent with the position; and
17. The Inventory Committee must be created also to prepare the RPCLRN and assess the condition of the local road at every reporting date, usually not later than January 31 of each year.

18. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to faithfully discharge their duties and functions in accordance with laws, rules, and regulations afore quoted and thereby submit the subject disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update these registries as per provisions outlined in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
20. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been assessed for RPT;
21. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year;
22. Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set up the RPT/SET Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.
26. Ensure the full disclosure policy is exercised and observed on the PPA’s for the 20% development fund and other funds for transparency and accountability purposes;
28. Submit regularly the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPAs on the 20% development fund.

29. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the annual MRRDFIP for the projects and activities approved plans including those projects intended for the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.
30. transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of MDRRF to STF;
31. submit the report on the utilization of the MDRRF fund regularly within the 15th day after the end of the month;
32. We further recommended that Management ensure the utilization of MDRRMF is in line with MDRRMFIP and conformity with laws rules and regulations.

33. We recommended that Management ensure that GAD funds are judicially and efficiently utilized and further substantially supported with GAD-related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation reports in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

34. It was further recommended that GAD required reports for monitoring and evaluation must be duly prepared and furnished to COA in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

36. To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially supported with GAD-related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation reports in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

37. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the general and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112, and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

39. We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards under Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII, and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

1.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountable officers to immediately submit the reports with complete
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to substantiate the
reliability and accuracy of accounts as presented in financial
statements as of year-end.

2.5 We therefore recommended that:

a) The LCE immediately create the Inventory Committee,
composed of adequate number of members, and complete the
physical count in three (3) months or less and submit a report
thereon. The Inventory Committee shall have at least one
member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units
of the agency;
b) The Inventory Committee plan/strategize on how to conduct and
complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It
shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) duly approved by
the Mayor, containing the specific assignments/duties of the
Committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying
the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from
start up to the targeted completion of physical inventory;
c) The Inventory Committee submit the approved PIP to the COA
Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the schedule start of
inventory; and
d) Property records will be updated by the General Services Officer
based on the result of the physical count and reconciled with
accounting records to come up with correct balances of PPE
accounts.

3.6 We recommended that Management create a special account in the
General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by
the local sanggunian pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 to easily
distinguish and track the utilization of funds related to COVID-19.

4.4 We recommended that Management prepare a monthly report on
BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs in
pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125.

5.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers related to the BGMC fund to avoid the receipt
of notices of suspension and possible disallowances of the transactions.

6.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning Development Officer to
prepare the supplemental AIP for the COVID-19-related PPAs and
the corresponding supplemental Appropriation Ordinance, duly
approved by the local Sanggunian, in support to the utilization of
BGCM fund.

7.7 We recommended that Honorable Municipal Mayor:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain necessary ledger
cards pursuant to MNGAS for LGUs, including the
Construction in Progress Ledger Cards;
b) Immediately appoint or designate a capable and permanent
General Service Officer who shall take charge of the duties and
responsibilities inherent with the position; and
c) Instruct the aforementioned Offices to make a periodic
reconciliation between the ledger cards and property cards to
support the accuracy of accounts presented in the financial
statements and make the data available at any given time.

8.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the:

a) The Municipal Engineer must provide the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
b) The Municipal Accountant must update and maintain the
general and subsidiary ledger of LRN and diligently compute
lapsing schedule and computation of the depreciation and
impairment loss of each component at the end of the year and
include in the financial statement proper disclosure thereof.
c) The GSO must maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map and prepare and submit the RLRN every end of the year; and
d) The Inventory Committee must be created also to prepare the
RPCLRN and assess the condition of the local road at every
reporting date, usually not later than January 31 of each year.

9.2 We recommended that:

a) Prior to payment of claims, the Management strictly comply
with documentary requirements needed on common
government transactions as embodied in COA Circular 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid suspensions and/or
disallowances in audit; and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws,
rules and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of
COA Circular No.

5. We recommended that LGU must be mindful on their targeted development plan in the uses of fund and accomplishment report must be closely monitored and regularly submitted/posted as required for transparency and good governance. They must be aware of the main objective of their development programs and what to focus to achieve a lasting economical, social, environmentally developed municipality and uplift the living conditions of their constituents.

6. We recommended that Management follow the LDRRM existing rules and regulations in the reporting, booking and utilization of the LDRRM Funds. The operational structures, system and policies must be strongly in place and followed and applied in case of disaster. All mitigation of disaster, starting from preparedness, information dissemination, knowing the extent of damage and number of victims and their needs, procurement and distribution of aid must be well documented and properly reported.

7. To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially supported with GAD related reports as monitoring and evaluation report as in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

8. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

9. We recommended that Management ensure that casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

10. We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

13. Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.

15. We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment. Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

17. We recommended that the management:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail.
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.

19. We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

20. We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.

21. We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.

22. We recommended:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail.
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
28. Advice the Local Chief Executive to initiate or to solicit fund from any legal sources for the completion of the said construction project.

29. Advice the agency head to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

30. The Chief Executive shall properly managed the proper utilization of public funds toned at the top to avoid the risk of misapplication of funds due to the withdrawal of funds from the bank upon credited by the bank and remiss in complying with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.

31. The Accountable Officer is directed to stamp “PAID” all paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents attached thereto after payments are made on such vouchers to preclude their possible re-use.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties
of the municipality; and
b) Require the submission of a complete Report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE).

2.5 We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to faithfully discharge their duties
and functions in accordance with laws, rules and regulation afore quoted
above and thereby submit the subject disbursement vouchers and its
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
period.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the
Municipal Accountant to maintain and update these registries as per
provisions set-forth in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of Manual on NGAS
for LGUs.

4.6 We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an
Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have
been assessed for RPT;
b) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant of a duly certified list
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year; and
c) Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set-up the RPT/SET Receivable
accounts due and collectible for the year.

5.9 We recommended that:
a. Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency must institutionalize
the ICS;
b. Management compel the Municipal Officers to act and do their
respective duties and functions strictly in accordance with laws, rules
and regulations; and
c. Direct the strict adherence in the application of internal control
measures for an efficient and effective conduct of the operation of the
Municipality towards development.

6.5 We recommended that Management:
a) Ensure the full disclosure policy is exercised and observed on the
PPA’s for the 20% development fund and other funds for
transparency and accountability purpose;
b) Submit at the start of the year, the list of all on-going government
PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with
complete details; and
c) Submit regularly the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPA’s
on 20% development fund.

7.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to
prepare and submit the annual MRRDFIP for the projects and activities
listed in approved plans including those projects intended for the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with section 5.1.2 of COA Circular
2012-002.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of MDRRF to STF and
b) submit the report on utilization of the MDRRF fund regularly within
15th day after the end of the month; and

8.7 We further recommended that Management ensure the utilization of
MDRRMF is in line with MDRRMFIP and in conformity with laws rules and
regulations.

9.6 We recommended that Management ensure that GAD funds are judicially
and efficiently utilized and implemented to achieve the GAD Program
objectives and gender mainstream. It was further recommended that
GAD required reports for monitoring and evaluation must be duly prepared
and furnished COA in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer must effect a deduction of an accurate mandatory
contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and withheld taxes and
further remit them within prescribed period of remittances.

11.4 We recommended that:
a. prior to payment of claims, Management strictly adhere to COA
Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary
requirements needed on common government transactions to avoid
suspensions and/or disallowances in audit, and
b. Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. Management must keep intact the Requisition and Issue Vouchers including the logbook records wherein all the accountable forms are listed and the name of person/AO who shall be accountable. This is also done for proper booking the value of the accountable forms and accounting the issuances and releases.
2. We recommended that the Accountable Officer must submit the original copy of the cancelled checks and/or Official Receipt in the Report of Checks Issued and/or Report of Collections and Deposits for proper validation of cancelled forms.
3. Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms must be regularly submitted for proper validation, easier tracking of the used and unused accountable forms and avoid possible loss and misuses of the accountable forms.
4. The serial number of checks issued must be reported and arranged in chronological manner in the RCI, Cashbook-Cash in Bank, and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and other reports for proper accounting and easier validation of documents and monitoring the existence of the accountable forms.

11. We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

14. We recommended that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made. For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.

18. It is recommended that top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal accountability by:
a) Demonstrating leadership by example;
b) Training personnel to gain work competence and or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person.
c) Maintaining a complete set of up-to-date records, namely: Journals; Ledgers; Registries; and Cash records.

25. Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration.
26. Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
27. Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.

We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

observations.recommendation: Advice the Local Chief Executive to initiate or to solicit fund from any legal sources for the completion of the said construction project.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented

observations.managementAction: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: The Chief Executive shall properly managed the utilization of public funds toned at the top to avoid the risk of misapplication of funds due to the withdrawal of funds from the bank upon credited by the bank and remiss in complying with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented

observations.recommendation:
1.1.3 Management must keep intact the Requisition and Issue Vouchers
including the logbook records wherein all the accountable forms
are listed and the name of person/AO who shall be accountable.
This is also done for proper booking the value of the accountable
forms and accounting the issuances and releases.

1.2.3 We recommended that the Accountable Officer must submit the
original copy of the cancelled checks and/or Official Receipt in the
Report of Checks Issued and/or Report of Collections and
Deposits for proper validation of cancelled forms.

1.3.3 Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms must be
regularly submitted for proper validation, easier tracking of the
used and unused accountable forms and avoid possible loss and
misuses of the accountable forms.

1.4.3 The serial number of checks issued must be reported and
arranged in chronological manner in the RCI, Cashbook-Cash in
Bank, and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms
and other reports for proper accounting and easier validation of
documents and monitoring the existence of the accountable forms.

2.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a
thorough and complete physical count of all properties of the
municipality. Require the update of inventory reports every end of the
year. The accounting and property office must comply with the
prescribed policies and procedures in the recording, reporting and
maintenance of records of PPE account as well as proper valuation
thereof.

3.5 We recommended that LGU must be mindful on their targeted
development plan in the uses of fund and accomplishment report must be
closely monitored and regularly submitted/posted as required for
transparency and good governance. They must be aware of the main
objective of their development programs and what to focus to achieve a
lasting economical, social, political and environmentally developed
municipality and uplift the living conditions of their constituents.

4.17 We recommended that Management follow the LDRRM existing rules
and regulations in the reporting, booking and utilization of the LDRRM
Funds. The operational structures, system and policies must be strongly
in place and followed and applied in case of disaster. All activities related
to mitigation of disaster, starting from preparedness, information
dissemination, knowing the extent of damage and number of victims and
their needs, procurement and distribution of aid must be well
documented and properly reported.

5.6 To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we
recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and
further substantially supported with GAD related reports as well as
monitoring and evaluation report as in compliance with PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

6.6 We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
continuously effect the timely and accurate mandatory contributions to
GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and
period of remittances. The discrepancies on the previous year related
accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile
to present an accurate status of contribution and remittance. Complete
employees ledger cards must be set-up and maintained for an easier
reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and
the employees share.

7.4 We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate settlement of the disallowances and suspensions pursuant to
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. It
should be emphasized that prior to payment of claims, that management
must strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for
the documentary requirements needed on common government
transactions before payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in
audit.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only required on revenue accounts but on accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to submit their required reports and monitor the date of submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will have meeting with Municipal Accountant and instructed her to thoroughly review the financial statements and observed the prescribed rules in reporting the financial operations of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will have meeting Municipal employees and the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of SWM Board that can focus the main goal of proper disposal of garbage in the locality.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by: a. demonstrating leadership by example; b. training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and c. enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.
observations.managementAction: The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to submit their required reports and monitor the date of submission.

observations.recommendation: Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had a meeting with the office of the Municipal Accountant and instructed her to thoroughly review the financial statements and observed the prescribed rules in reporting the financial operations of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made. For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the Accountant for the strict observation of "no DTR no Salary" and thoroughly check the payrolls before processing payments of salaries.

observations.recommendation: Management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) protecting women’s human rights; and (b) promoting women’s economic empowerment. Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the accountant and the GAD Focal person for the proper alignment of funding of GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS defining therein the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, and the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof. The following steps may help in facilitating reconciliation of records: a. Secure detailed profile of payables per GSIS records; b. Reconstruct old GSIS accounts, if necessary, and update records; c. Reconstruct old personnel profile, if necessary, and update personnel profile; d. Counter-check GSIS records with the reconstructed and updated accounting and personnel records of the LGU; e. Negotiate with the GSIS for any differences that may be noted during counter-checking activity; f. Enter into a formal agreement with the GSIS in the form of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and g. Appropriate fund for the settlement of any payables to the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the records and process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement. Actual rate of deductions and remittances must be effect for the regular and current personnel of the municipality.
observations.reason: Working back of the previous year's records is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that management: must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate the internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community; make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail; provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: Apprehension due to the remoteness of municipality and security reason.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal accountability by: demonstrating leadership by example; training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person. Maintain a complete set of up-to-date records, namely: Journals; Ledgers; Registries; and Cash records.
observations.managementAction: The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the books and attend seminar.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to be invited.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: Most of the inhabitant are farmers and imposing tax on them is a financial burden and they ignore the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that management: must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate the internet publication of procurements; make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation; provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: Apprehension due to the remoteness of municipality and security reason.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management must make sure that the conditions and the requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration; must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: Apprehension due to the remoteness of municipality and security reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports of all offices/department every year end which must be further reconcile with the PPE account presented in the financial statement.  Adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance of PPE per books and the property/equipment ledger card.
observations.managementAction: The LCE  directed the accountant and GSO for the proper reconciliation of PPE per books and consider the dropping in books of properties which are  no longer exist and coordinate with the COA for proper action on this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”
observations.managementAction: The LCE called the attention of MDRRMO and the accountant for the proper implementation of this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the accountant and t the GAD Focal person for the proper alignment of funding of GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management must find ways to post the bids and awards to PhilGEPS in compliance to the provisions in IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Management said that the remoteness of the municipality did not have an internet access and the in-charge are not computer letirate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer must submit within five (5) days after the end of each month, the monthly RCD together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books of accounts and to ensure that the said reports be submitted to the Auditor within ten (10) days from receipt.
observations.reason: Transactions was being work back from the source documents for proper recording and adjusting entries will be done if necessary.

observations.recommendation: Advice the Local Chief Executive to initiate or to solicit fund from any legal sources for the completion of the said construction project.
observations.reason: Management did not offer any explanation on this.

observations.recommendation: Advice the agency head to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Management did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: The Chief Executive shall properly managed the proper utilization of public funds toned at the top to avoid the risk of misapplication of funds due to the withdrawal of funds from the bank upon credited by the bank and remiss in complying with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.
observations.reason: The remoteness of the municipality from the service bank.

observations.recommendation: The Accountable Officer is directed to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents attached thereto after payments are made on such vouchers to preclude their possible re-use.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order responsible officials to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that Management ensure that payment of casuals/contractual are supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports every end of the year.   The accounting and property office must comply with the prescribed policies and procedures in the recording, reporting and maintenance of records of PPE account.

We recommended that the Top Management compel the accountable officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and accountability.  The Municipal Accountant must be required to submit promptly the periodic financial and other reports and together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor in accordance to pertinent laws, rules and regulations to ensure timely audit of transactions and verification of accounts.

We recommended that Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continuously effect a timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of remittances.  The discrepancies on the previous year related accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile to present an accurate status of contribution and remittance.  Complete employees ledger cards must be set-up and maintained for an easier reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.
We recommended that travel expenses must be treated as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that Management follow the LDRRM existing rules and regulations in the reporting, booking and utilization of the LDRRM Funds.  The operational structures, system and policies must be strongly in place and followed and applied in case of disaster.  All activities related to mitigation of disaster, starting from preparedness, information dissemination, knowing the extent of damage and number of victims and their needs, procurement and distribution of aid must be well documented and properly reported.

We recommended that Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

To strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU, we recommended that the Management use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

We recommended that Management submit the  List of PPAs to COA at the on-set of the year to promote transparency and good governance and stakeholders were be able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that officers/employees determined to be responsible submit immediately the required documents and/or payments for the settlement of the suspension/disallowances.  It should be emphasized that prior to payment of claims, that management must strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements needed on common government transactions before payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.

Furthermore, direct him to conduct a thorough inquiry and verification on the circumstances surrounding the existence of the long outstanding Inter-agency payables, and their proper disposition as prescribed by applicable COA regulations.

We recommend that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made.

For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

It is recommended that municipal government must determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

It is recommended that the management should review the deductions made from their employees and rate must be adjusted pursuant to applicable laws, rules and regulations on income tax and GSIS/Pag-ibig/PhilHealth premium contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports of all offices/department every year end which must be further reconcile with the PPE account presented in the financial statement.  Adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance of PPE per books and the property/equipment ledger card.

We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”

We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, and Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.  Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.

We recommended the immediate creation of an inventory committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality and require update of inventory reports of all offices/department as of December 31, 2015.  We further recommended that the inventory reports must be reconciled with the reported PPE accounts and adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance per books.
observations.managementAction: Had created an Inventory Committee  and  physical inventory had been initially conducted and reconciliation is still on-going.
observations.reason: Previous records of the actual cost and book value of PPE were being workout on files from past administration.  The absence of records on file made it hard to reconcile the PPE accounts.

observations.reason: Working back of the previous year’s records of personnel, determining who are still employed and those who are no longer connected/ separated since when.

Proper implementation of the plan involving the community will result into a clean and healthy public and environment.
observations.reason: Created Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, who will prepare the 10 year Local Solid Waste Management Plan but it was not yet submitted.

We recommended and management agreed that:
They must be oriented on laws, rules and regulations concerning hiring/employment of contractual workers and strictly adhere thereof. 
Specifically, make sure that claims for wages of casual and contractual workers are duly supported with necessary papers pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 and other pertinent guidelines.  
Moreover, be guided with the provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement of manpower which provides under Appendix I (3.7) thereof that the manual labor component of projects undertaken by administration may be undertaken (1) in-house (personnel occupying regular plantilla), (2) by job order or through the pakyaw contracting system.
observations.reason: Management reasoned that hiring their people is part of helping them.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended and management agreed that:
Cause for the conduct orientation among concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01; 
Specifically, make sure that rules and regulation on proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with; and
GAD Fund must only be utilized according to the intended purpose(s).
observations.reason: Management reasoned that they have always been catering the needs of the women folks and the children.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.
observations.reason: They did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.
observations.reason: Most of the inhabitant are farmers and imposing tax on them is a financial burden and they ignore the Municipal assessor.
Lack capable manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned accountable officer must issue a corresponding Official Receipt for all revenues and other income received and properly submit the corresponding Report of Collections and Deposits.
observations.reason: Lack of capable staff.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within 5-day after its perfection/approval/execution
observations.reason: No explanation had been offered on this.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that management:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.  
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail. 
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.reason: Apprehension due to the remoteness of the municipality and security reason.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/ construction of any project to be undertaken by administration.
Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.reason: Apprehension due to the remoteness of the municipality and security reason.

observations.recommendation: Management accountable officers must submit promptly the reports and the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents to facilitate the complete review and evaluation and to affirm the reliability on the existence/occurrence, validity, accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions.
observations.reason: Responsible officer lack of knowledge and training in accounting.
